revision.comment: 新しいページ：両宮山古墳群（岡山県赤磐市にある古墳群）。
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=両宮山
古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|両宮山古墳群の位置}}}} '''両宮山古墳群'''（りょうぐんざんこふんぐん）は、[[岡山県]][[赤磐市]]にある[[古墳群]]。一部が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。「'''西高月古墳群'''（にしたかつきこふんぐん）」とも。 == 概要 == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[両宮山古墳]]}}]] 岡山県南部、吉備地方南東部の備前地方に分布する古墳群である。[[前方後円墳]]4基・[[帆立貝形古墳]]2基などから構成され、主墳の[[両宮山古墳]]は岡山県第3位の規模の巨大古墳になる。周辺では、後世に[[備前国分寺跡|備前国分寺]]も営まれている。 古墳群のうち、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）に両宮山古墳の古墳域が国の[[史跡]]に指定され、[[1978年]]（昭和53年）に和田茶臼山古墳の古墳域が史跡「両宮山古墳」に追加指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2251|両宮山古墳}}。また[[2007年]]（[[平成]]19年）には、小山古墳の石棺が赤磐市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 一覧 == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ 両宮山古墳群（西高月古墳群）の主な古墳の一覧両宮山古墳パンフレット（2016年）。[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html 周辺の古墳]（赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」）。 ! 古墳名 ! 所在地 ! 座標 ! 形状 ! 墳丘長 ! 時期 ! 史跡指定 ! 備考 |- | '''[[両宮山古墳]]'''
（りょうぐうざんこふん） | [[赤磐市]]穂崎・和田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=両宮山古墳}} | [[前方後円墳]] | 206m | 5c後半 | 国の史跡 | 古墳総長349メートル（周濠を含む全長）
岡山県第3位の規模の巨大古墳 |- | '''和田茶臼山古墳'''
（わだちゃうすやまこふん） | 赤磐市和田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=和田茶臼山古墳}} | [[帆立貝形古墳]] | 55m | 5c後半-末 | 国の史跡 | 史跡「両宮山古墳」に包含
両宮山古墳の陪塚か |- | '''森山古墳'''
（もりやまこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=森山古墳}} | 帆立貝形古墳 | 82m | 5c後半 | なし | 両宮山古墳に続く首長墓か
北側の正免東古墳（非現存）は陪塚か |- | '''廻り山古墳'''
（まわりやまこふん） | 赤磐市岩田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=廻り山古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 47m | 6c前半 | なし | |- | '''朱千駄古墳'''
（しゅせんだこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=朱千駄古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 85m | 5c末 | なし | 竜山石製の組合式[[長持形石棺]]の出土
（現在は[[岡山県立博物館]]展示）
副葬品出土 |- | '''小山古墳'''
（こやまこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=小山古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 67m | 5c末 | なし | 後円部墳頂に阿蘇溶結凝灰岩製の古式家形石棺
（赤磐市指定有形文化財）[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/42/44/69/67/68.html 小山古墳石棺]（赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」）。 |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|和田茶臼山古墳 File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|森山古墳 File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|廻り山古墳 File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|朱千駄古墳 File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|小山古墳 File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|小山古墳の石棺片 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 各古墳の史跡説明板（赤磐市教育委員会設置） * パンフレット「国指定史跡 両宮山古墳」（岡山県赤磐市教育委員会、2016年） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「両宮山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「西森山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「廻山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「朱千駄古墳」|ref={{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「小山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html 両宮山古墳]、[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html 周辺の古墳] - 赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」 {{DEFAULTSORT:りようくうさんこふんくん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:赤磐市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

{{small|[[岡山県立博物館]]玄関前展示。}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|小山古墳 File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|小山古墳の石棺片 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 各古墳の史跡説明板（赤磐市教育委員会設置） * パンフレット「国指定史跡 両宮山古墳」（岡山県赤磐市教育委員会、2016年） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「両宮山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「西森山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「廻山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「朱千駄古墳」|ref={{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「小山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html 両宮山古墳]、[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html 周辺の古墳] - 赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」 {{DEFAULTSORT:りようくうさんこふんくん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:赤磐市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=両宮山
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|両宮山古墳群の位置}}}} '''両宮山古墳群'''（りょうぐんざんこふんぐん）は、[[岡山県]][[赤磐市]]にある[[古墳群]]。一部が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。「'''西高月古墳群'''（にしたかつきこふんぐん）」とも。 == 概要 == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[両宮山古墳]]}}]] 岡山県南部、吉備地方南東部の備前地方に分布する古墳群である。[[前方後円墳]]4基・[[帆立貝形古墳]]2基などから構成され、主墳の[[両宮山古墳]]は岡山県第3位の規模の巨大古墳になる。周辺では、後世に[[備前国分寺跡|備前国分寺]]も営まれている。 古墳群のうち、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）に両宮山古墳の古墳域が国の[[史跡]]に指定され、[[1978年]]（昭和53年）に和田茶臼山古墳の古墳域が史跡「両宮山古墳」に追加指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2251|両宮山古墳}}。また[[2007年]]（[[平成]]19年）には、小山古墳の石棺が赤磐市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 一覧 == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ 両宮山古墳群（西高月古墳群）の主な古墳の一覧両宮山古墳パンフレット（2016年）。[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html 周辺の古墳]（赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」）。 ! 古墳名 ! 所在地 ! 座標 ! 形状 ! 墳丘長 ! 築造時期 ! 史跡指定 ! 備考 |- | '''[[両宮山古墳]]'''
（りょうぐうざんこふん） | [[赤磐市]]穂崎・和田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=両宮山古墳}} | [[前方後円墳]] | 206m | 5c後半 | 国の史跡 | 古墳総長349メートル（周濠を含む全長）
岡山県第3位の規模の巨大古墳 |- | '''和田茶臼山古墳'''
（わだちゃうすやまこふん） | 赤磐市和田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=和田茶臼山古墳}} | [[帆立貝形古墳]] | 55m | 5c後半-末 | 国の史跡 | 史跡「両宮山古墳」に包含
両宮山古墳の陪塚か |- | '''森山古墳'''
（もりやまこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=森山古墳}} | 帆立貝形古墳 | 82m | 5c後半 | なし | 両宮山古墳に続く首長墓か
北側の正免東古墳（非現存）は陪塚か |- | '''廻り山古墳'''
（まわりやまこふん） | 赤磐市岩田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=廻り山古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 47m | 6c前半 | なし | |- | '''朱千駄古墳'''
（しゅせんだこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=朱千駄古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 85m | 5c末 | なし | 竜山石製の組合式[[長持形石棺]]の出土
（現在は[[岡山県立博物館]]展示）
副葬品出土 |- | '''小山古墳'''
（こやまこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=小山古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 67m | 5c末 | なし | 後円部墳頂に阿蘇溶結凝灰岩製の古式家形石棺
（赤磐市指定有形文化財）[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/42/44/69/67/68.html 小山古墳石棺]（赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」）。 |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|和田茶臼山古墳 File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|森山古墳 File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|廻り山古墳 File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|朱千駄古墳 ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|朱千駄古墳出土 [[長持形石棺]]
{{small|[[岡山県立博物館]]玄関前展示。}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|小山古墳 File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|小山古墳の石棺片 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 各古墳の史跡説明板（赤磐市教育委員会設置） * パンフレット「国指定史跡 両宮山古墳」（岡山県赤磐市教育委員会、2016年） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「両宮山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「西森山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「廻山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「朱千駄古墳」|ref={{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「小山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html 両宮山古墳]、[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html 周辺の古墳] - 赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」 {{DEFAULTSORT:りようくうさんこふんくん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:赤磐市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

（赤磐市指定有形文化財）[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/42/44/69/67/68.html 小山古墳石棺]（赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」）。 |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|和田茶臼山古墳 File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|森山古墳 File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|廻り山古墳 File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|朱千駄古墳 ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|朱千駄古墳出土 [[長持形石棺]]

{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=両宮山
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|両宮山古墳群の位置}}}} '''両宮山古墳群'''（りょうぐうざんこふんぐん）は、[[岡山県]][[赤磐市]]にある[[古墳群]]。2基が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。「西高月古墳群（にしたかつきこふんぐん）」とも。 == 概要 == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[両宮山古墳]]}}]] 岡山県南部、吉備地方南東部の備前地方に分布する古墳群である。[[前方後円墳]]4基・[[帆立貝形古墳]]2基などから構成され、主墳の[[両宮山古墳]]は岡山県第3位の規模の巨大古墳になる。周辺では、後世に[[備前国分寺跡|備前国分寺]]も営まれている。 古墳群のうち、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）に両宮山古墳の古墳域が国の[[史跡]]に指定され、[[1978年]]（昭和53年）に和田茶臼山古墳の古墳域が史跡「両宮山古墳」に追加指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2251|両宮山古墳}}。また[[2007年]]（[[平成]]19年）には、小山古墳の石棺が赤磐市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 一覧 == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ 両宮山古墳群（西高月古墳群）の主な古墳の一覧両宮山古墳パンフレット（2016年）。[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html 周辺の古墳]（赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」）。 ! 古墳名 ! 所在地 ! 座標 ! 形状 ! 墳丘長 ! 築造時期 ! 史跡指定 ! 備考 |- | '''[[両宮山古墳]]'''
（りょうぐうざんこふん） | [[赤磐市]]穂崎・和田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=両宮山古墳}} | [[前方後円墳]] | 206m | 5c後半 | 国の史跡 | 古墳総長349メートル（周濠を含む全長）
岡山県第3位の規模の巨大古墳 |- | '''和田茶臼山古墳'''
（わだちゃうすやまこふん） | 赤磐市和田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=和田茶臼山古墳}} | [[帆立貝形古墳]] | 55m | 5c後半-末 | 国の史跡 | 史跡「両宮山古墳」に包含
両宮山古墳の陪塚か |- | '''森山古墳'''
（もりやまこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=森山古墳}} | 帆立貝形古墳 | 82m | 5c後半 | なし | 両宮山古墳に続く首長墓か
北側の正免東古墳（非現存）は陪塚か |- | '''廻り山古墳'''
（まわりやまこふん） | 赤磐市岩田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=廻り山古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 47m | 6c前半 | なし | |- | '''朱千駄古墳'''
（しゅせんだこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=朱千駄古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 85m | 5c末 | なし | 竜山石製の組合式[[長持形石棺]]の出土
（現在は[[岡山県立博物館]]展示）
副葬品出土 |- | '''小山古墳'''
（こやまこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=小山古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 67m | 5c末 | なし | 後円部墳頂に阿蘇溶結凝灰岩（阿蘇石）製の古式家形石棺
（赤磐市指定有形文化財）[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/42/44/69/67/68.html 小山古墳石棺]（赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」）。 |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|和田茶臼山古墳 File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|森山古墳 File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|廻り山古墳 File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|朱千駄古墳 ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|朱千駄古墳出土 [[長持形石棺]]
{{small|[[岡山県立博物館]]玄関前展示。}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|小山古墳 File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|小山古墳の石棺片 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 各古墳の史跡説明板（赤磐市教育委員会設置） * パンフレット「国指定史跡 両宮山古墳」（岡山県赤磐市教育委員会、2016年） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「両宮山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「西森山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「廻山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「朱千駄古墳」|ref={{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「小山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html 両宮山古墳]、[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html 周辺の古墳] - 赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」 {{DEFAULTSORT:りようくうさんこふんくん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:赤磐市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=両宮山
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|両宮山古墳群の位置}}}} '''両宮山古墳群'''（りょうぐうざんこふんぐん）は、[[岡山県]][[赤磐市]]にある[[古墳群]]。2基が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。「西高月古墳群（にしたかつきこふんぐん）」とも。 == 概要 == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[両宮山古墳]]}}]] 岡山県南部、吉備地方南東部の備前地方に分布する古墳群である。[[前方後円墳]]4基・[[帆立貝形古墳]]2基などから構成され、主墳の[[両宮山古墳]]は岡山県第3位の規模の巨大古墳になる。周辺では、後世に[[備前国分寺跡|備前国分寺]]も営まれている。 古墳群のうち、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）に両宮山古墳の古墳域が国の[[史跡]]に指定され、[[1978年]]（昭和53年）に和田茶臼山古墳の古墳域が史跡「両宮山古墳」に追加指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2251|両宮山古墳}}。また[[2007年]]（[[平成]]19年）には、小山古墳の石棺が赤磐市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 一覧 == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ 両宮山古墳群（西高月古墳群）の主な古墳の一覧両宮山古墳パンフレット（2016年）。[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html 周辺の古墳]（赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」）。 ! 古墳名 ! 所在地 ! 座標 ! 形状 ! 墳丘長 ! 築造時期 ! 史跡指定 ! 備考 |- | '''[[両宮山古墳]]'''
（りょうぐうざんこふん） | [[赤磐市]]穂崎・和田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=両宮山古墳}} | [[前方後円墳]] | 206m | 5c後半 | 国の史跡 | 古墳総長349メートル（周濠を含む全長）
岡山県第3位の規模の巨大古墳 |- | '''和田茶臼山古墳'''
（わだちゃうすやまこふん） | 赤磐市和田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=和田茶臼山古墳}} | [[帆立貝形古墳]] | 55m | 5c後半-末 | 国の史跡 | 史跡「両宮山古墳」に包含
両宮山古墳の陪塚か |- | '''森山古墳'''
（もりやまこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=森山古墳}} | 帆立貝形古墳 | 82m | 5c後半 | なし | 両宮山古墳に続く首長墓か
北側の正免東古墳（非現存）は陪塚か |- | '''廻り山古墳'''
（まわりやまこふん） | 赤磐市岩田 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=廻り山古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 47m | 6c前半 | なし | |- | '''朱千駄古墳'''
（しゅせんだこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=朱千駄古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 85m | 5c末 | なし | 竜山石製の組合式[[長持形石棺]]の出土
（現在は[[岡山県立博物館]]展示）
副葬品出土 |- | '''小山古墳'''
（こやまこふん） | 赤磐市穂崎 | {{ウィキ座標|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=小山古墳}} | 前方後円墳 | 67m | 5c末 | なし | 後円部墳頂に阿蘇溶結凝灰岩（阿蘇石）製の古式家形石棺
（赤磐市指定有形文化財）[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/42/44/69/67/68.html 小山古墳石棺]（赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」）。 |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|和田茶臼山古墳 File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|森山古墳 File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|廻り山古墳 File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|朱千駄古墳 ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|朱千駄古墳出土 [[長持形石棺]]
{{small|[[岡山県立博物館]]玄関前展示。}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|小山古墳 File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|小山古墳の石棺片 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 各古墳の史跡説明板（赤磐市教育委員会設置） * パンフレット「国指定史跡 両宮山古墳」（岡山県赤磐市教育委員会、2016年） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「両宮山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「西森山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「廻山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「朱千駄古墳」|ref={{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「小山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html 両宮山古墳]、[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html 周辺の古墳] - 赤磐市教育委員会「赤磐の文化財」 {{DEFAULTSORT:りようくうさんこふんくん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:赤磐市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubunji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ** Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubunji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ** Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubunji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ** Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubunji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Shusenda Kofun]] to [[Mori-yama Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Mori-yama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''

Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. {{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Mori-yama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mori-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun ファイル:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mori-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusenda Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mori-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]]
