revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - revision.comment: Adding [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! revision.comment: Text replacement - "|史跡}" to "|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}"
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:kkkk]]" to ""
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''

Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} from Shusenda Kofun

Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} from Shusenda Kofun

{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} from Shusenda Kofun

{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{座標一覧}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References
