revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiwa|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National {{ill|Historic Sites (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōgūzan Kofun|qq=}}}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōgūzan Kofun|qq=}}'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiwa|qq=}}, Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Zenpō-kōenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|両宮山古墳群|version=85170626|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Mori-yama Kofun]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]] [[en:Mori-yama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|両宮山古墳群|version=85170626|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Mori-yama Kofun]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]] [[en:Mori-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:赤磐市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|両宮山古墳群|version=85170626|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Mori-yama Kofun]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:赤磐市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kofun in Okayama Prefecture
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|両宮山古墳群|version=85170626|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] }} [[sn:Mori-yama Kofun]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:赤磐市の歴史]]

Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:赤磐の Municipal History]] into [[Category:History of Akaiwa City]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|両宮山古墳群|version=85170626|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] }} [[sn:Mori-yama Kofun]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa City]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Scallop Kofun" to "scallop-shaped kofun"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28692354]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun

It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|両宮山古墳群|version=85170626|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] }} [[sn:Mori-yama Kofun]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa City]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Mori-yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|両宮山古墳群|version=85170626|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] }} [[sn:Mori-yama Kofun]] [[ja:両宮山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa City]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Mori-yama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: append |1= |comment= |WD= in {{ill}}
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28692354]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡|1=Bizen Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387321}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28692354]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡|1=Bizen Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387321}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|両宮山古墳群|version=85170626|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] }} [[ja:両宮山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa City]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q28692354}}

In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28692354]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡|1=Bizen Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387321}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''

A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''

[[ja:両宮山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28692354]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡|1=Bizen Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387321}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
(こやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|3.65|N|134|0|33.80|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Koyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 67m | Late 5th century | None | Features a sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff. Designated as an Akaiwa City Tangible Cultural Property.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} |} File:Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun File:Moriyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Moriyama Kofun File:Mawariyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Mawariyama Kofun File:Shusenda Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Shusenda Kofun File:朱千駄古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|{{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} from Shusenda Kofun
{{small|Displayed at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.}} File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), zenkei.jpg|Koyama Kofun File:Koyama Kofun (Akaiwa), sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus fragment from Koyama Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Description plaques at each kofun (installed by Akaiwa City Board of Education) * Pamphlet: "National Historic Site Ryōgūzan Kofun" (Akaiwa City Board of Education, 2016) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref=}} ''' Includes entries: {{Harvid|両宮山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|西森山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|廻山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|朱千駄古墳（平凡社）|1988}}, {{Harvid|小山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}. == External Links == * {{National Important Cultural Property Database|401|2251|両宮山古墳}} * [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37.html Ryōgūzan Kofun], [http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Nearby Kofun] - Akaiwa City Cultural Heritage Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|両宮山古墳群|version=85170626|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] }} [[ja:両宮山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa City]] {{wikidata link|Q28692354}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
[[ja:両宮山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28692354]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=23.20|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=9.36|label=Ryōgūzan
Kofungun|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of the Ryōgūzan Kofun Group}}}} '''Ryōgūzan Kofun Group''' (両宮山古墳群, Ryōgūzan Kofungun) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Okayama Prefecture. Two of the mounds are designated as National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. It is also referred to as the "Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group" (西高月古墳群). == Overview == [[File:Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]}}]] This kofun group is situated in the southeastern Bizen area of the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, southern Okayama Prefecture. It comprises four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds) and two {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (scallop-shaped mounds), with the main mound, Ryōgūzan Kofun, Ranking as the third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. The area also includes the remnants of the later {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja|備前国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kokubun-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国分寺跡|1=Bizen Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387321}}. In 1927, the area surrounding Ryōgūzan Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site. In 1978, the area of Wada Chausuyama Kofun was added to the designation under "Ryōgūzan Kofun."{{Sfn|国指定}} Additionally, in 2007, the sarcophagus from Koyama Kofun was designated as an Important Tangible Cultural Property by Akaiwa City.{{Sfn|小山古墳石棺}} == List == {{GeoGroup}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ Ryōgūzan Kofun Group (Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun Group) - Main Kofun ListRyōgūzan Kofun Pamphlet (2016).[http://bunkazai.akaiwa-rekishi.jp/37/205.html Kofun in the Area] (Akaiwa City Board of Education, "Cultural Assets of Akaiwa"). ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Mound Length ! Construction Period ! Historic Site Designation ! Notes |- | '''[[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūzan Kofun]]'''
(りょうぐうざんこふん) | [[:en:Akaiwa|Akaiwa]], Hosaki and Wada | {{Coord|34|44|23.20|N|134|0|9.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Ryōgūzan Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 206m | Late 5th century | National Historic Site | Total length: 349m (including moats). Third-largest kofun in Okayama Prefecture. |- | '''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''
(わだちゃうすやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Wada | {{Coord|34|44|29.40|N|134|0|6.11|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Wada Chausuyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 55m | Late 5th century to early 6th century | National Historic Site | Included in the "Ryōgūzan Kofun" designation. Likely an associated satellite mound. |- | '''Moriyama Kofun'''
(もりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|16.66|N|134|0|17.66|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Moriyama Kofun}} | {{ill|Hotategaigata kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | 82m | Late 5th century | None | Potential successor to Ryōgūzan Kofun. A satellite mound (Shōmen East Kofun) no longer exists. |- | '''Mawariyama Kofun'''
(まわりやまこふん) | Akaiwa, Iwata | {{Coord|34|44|19.52|N|134|0|27.37|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Mawariyama Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 47m | Early 6th century | None | |- | '''Shusenda Kofun'''
(しゅせんだこふん) | Akaiwa, Hosaki | {{Coord|34|44|1.06|N|133|59|57.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Shusenda Kofun}} | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | 85m | Late 5th century | None | Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Ryūzan stone. Now exhibited at the Okayama Prefectural Museum. |- | '''Koyama Kofun'''
